title: EU greenlights controversial return hubs in 'strictest-ever' new migration law
content: A new EU law will allow migrant return hubs outside the bloc and home searches in a bid to crack down on irregular migration. Civil society groups point to a “xenophobic" turn in EU politics.
snippet: A new EU law will allow migrant return hubs outside the bloc and home searches in a bid to crack down on irregular migration. Civil society groups point to a “xenophobic" turn in EU politics.

title: Crowning of PSG: an evening of celebrations at the Parc des Princes
content: Euronews went to the Parc des Princes on Sunday evening, where the PSG was celebrating its victory of the Champions League, to take the pulse of the fans.
snippet: Euronews went to the Parc des Princes on Sunday evening, where the PSG was celebrating its victory of the Champions League, to take the pulse of the fans.

title: Hungary's Péter Magyar heads to Berlin and Paris to seal EU reset
content: Hungary's PM Péter Magyar heads to Germany and France this week as part of his push to return Budapest to the European mainstream. The trip follows a deal with Brussels to unlock €16.4bn in frozen EU funds.

title: Blue Origin explosion ‘huge setback for everyone in space community’, ESA chief says
content: The European Space Agency’s (ESA) chief told Euronews that the explosion of a Blue Origin New Glenn rocket, which plays a crucial role in NASA’s Artemis missions to return to the Moon, will cause significant delays.

title: EU says drones are a threat but scrambles for common response
content: EU countries agree drone threats require stronger coordination, but divisions remain over information sharing and national control. Amid fibrillations on the bloc’s eastern flank, a leaked document highlights efforts to boost detection, resilience and cooperation while avoiding overlap with NATO.

title: Hungary: Tamás Sulyok will not resign as president
content: The government’s deadline for his resignation passed on Sunday night, but Sulyok said he would wait for the Venice Commission’s opinion and insisted that the matter should be handled in line with the constitution.

title: 'Crime hotspots': how to tackle knife attacks and other violence at German stations?
content: Politicians from the SPD and CDU also lament that many people do not feel safe at Germany's railway stations – including staff. A criminologist explains why there is more violence there than elsewhere.

title: Russia launches fresh strikes on Ukraine, Kyiv hits refinery hub
content: In response to Russian shelling of Ukrainian territory, Ukrainian forces launched a series of powerful strikes on strategically important Russian oil infrastructure.
snippet: In response to Russian shelling of Ukrainian territory, Ukrainian forces launched a series of powerful strikes on strategically important Russian oil infrastructure.

title: Malta's Labour party wins historic fourth term in snap general election
content: Voters in Malta have granted the Labour Party a record-breaking fourth consecutive mandate, according to preliminary results published on Sunday. The result marks a fresh victory for Prime Minister Robert Abela, with early indications showing Labour comfortably ahead

title: Paris Saint-Germain wins the Champions League after penalty shootout victory against Arsenal
content: Paris Saint-Germain won back-to-back Champions League titles after a 4–3 penalty shootout victory over Arsenal in Budapest. Following a 1–1 draw through extra time, Gabriel Magalhães missed the decisive spot kick to hand PSG the trophy.

title: Austrian protesters shut Brenner motorway on Saturday over pollution
content: Thousands of demonstrators blocked Austria’s vital Brenner motorway on Saturday to protest rising traffic noise and pollution. The eight-hour shutdown of the transit corridor between Germany and Italy disrupted travel.
snippet: Thousands of demonstrators blocked Austria’s vital Brenner motorway on Saturday to protest rising traffic noise and pollution. The eight-hour shutdown of the transit corridor between Germany and Italy

title: Galați residents fear future incidents after Russian drone crashed into building
content: A Russian drone crashed into a residential building into the eastern Romanian city of Galați on Friday, sparking a fire to its roof and injuring two people.
snippet: A Russian drone crashed into a residential building into the eastern Romanian city of Galați on Friday, sparking a fire to its roof and injuring two people.

title: How autonomous machines are transforming road and tunnel safety

title: Maltese cast ballots as Labour government seeks fourth term in snap election
content: Maltese voters headed to the polls on Saturday for a snap election, with Prime Minister Robert Abela’s Labour Party tipped to win on a platform of economic stability and 4% GDP growth, despite ongoing corruption concerns.

title: Polish President Nawrocki seeks to strip Zelenskyy of Poland's highest honour
content: President Karol Nawrocki said he will ask for Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to be stripped of the White Eagle, after Zelenskyy named a military unit after a group that led massacres of ethnic Poles during WWII.

title: France sees as sharp rise in anti-Jewish, anti-Christian and anti-Muslim acts, says government
content: In a new report, the French Interior Ministry says that "anti-religious acts strike at freedom of conscience and the free exercise of religion" and endanger "the very balance of our society".
snippet: In a new report, the French Interior Ministry says that "anti-religious acts strike at freedom of conscience and the free exercise of religion" and endanger "the very balance of our society".

title: France launches an inquiry into the treatment of activists on the Gaza flotilla
content: The French Foreign Minister, Jean-Noël Barrot, announced on Friday that he had referred the matter to the courts following reports of humiliation, rape and acts of torture against activists on board the Gaza flotilla.

title: The drone crash is “the full responsibility of the Russian Federation”, says Romanian FM
content: Oana Toiu, Romania's foreign minister, confirmed to Euronews that the drone that struck Romania was of Russian origin and was carrying explosives.
snippet: Oana Toiu, Romania's foreign minister, confirmed to Euronews that the drone that struck Romania was of Russian origin and was carrying explosives.

title: Four EU countries push Brussels to ease carbon market pressure on industry
content: Estonia, France, Germany and Spain support the Emissions Trading System, the EU’s major climate policy tool, but want clearer, simpler and possibly more flexible rules to protect industrial competitiveness, according to a document seen by Euronews.

title: Can the EU de-risk from China and make its trade relationship sustainable?
content: The European Commission declared its trade relationship with China "not sustainable”, pushing for a tougher de-risking but not decoupling strategy. Is this a realistic plan?
snippet: The European Commission declared its trade relationship with China "not sustainable”, pushing for a tougher de-risking but not decoupling strategy. Is this a realistic plan?

title: Europe Today: US-Iran peace deal at risk as EU approves controversial migration law
content: Despite Israel's offensive in Lebanon, which prompted Iran to threaten to end its ceasefire with the US, Donald Trump says peace talks "will go on". Meanwhile in Europe, the EU has approved a controversial migration law allowing member states to establish return hubs in third countries.

title: France bans display of Israeli offensive weapons at Eurosatory arms fair
content: The Israeli defence ministry condemned it as a shameful decision, driven by political and commercial interests, and part of a deeply troubling trend in French behaviour in recent years.
snippet: The Israeli defence ministry condemned it as a shameful decision, driven by political and commercial interests, and part of a deeply troubling trend in French behaviour in recent years.

title: 'Football doesn't make us stupid': young French players challenge stereotypes at speaking contest
content: Every year, hundreds of aspiring footballers chase a different kind of win: a gold at a national speaking competition dedicated to young athletes.
snippet: Every year, hundreds of aspiring footballers chase a different kind of win: a gold at a national speaking competition dedicated to young athletes.

title: Sweden eases rules for teenage migrants facing deportation, migration minister says
content: Following a large influx of asylum seekers in Sweden in 2015, successive left- and right-wing governments have tightened asylum rules.
snippet: Following a large influx of asylum seekers in Sweden in 2015, successive left- and right-wing governments have tightened asylum rules.

title: Court rejects Rwanda's €115 million claim against UK over scrapped migrant deal
content: In 2022, former UK Prime Minister Boris Johnson sealed a deal with Kigali to send migrants arriving in Britain via "dangerous or illegal journeys" in small boats or lorries to Rwanda.
snippet: In 2022, former UK Prime Minister Boris Johnson sealed a deal with Kigali to send migrants arriving in Britain via "dangerous or illegal journeys" in small boats or lorries to Rwanda.

title: China's state subsidies up to eight times more than OECD's, report says
content: Chinese state subsidies have hit record levels, climbing to nearly 10% of company revenue in the chip sector and distorting markets, according to a new OECD report, published as the EU mulls new measures to counter Beijing's aid.

title: The EU is set to join US-led chip alliance 'Pax Silica' to counter China's AI race
content: The EU is set to formally join Pax Silica, a US-led initiative coordinating AI chip supply chains and export controls to counter China. The decision follows weeks of internal discussions over fears that the initiative could jeopardise the EU's regulatory autonomy.

title: Explainer: What is electrification – and why is the EU betting its future on it?
content: Electrifying the European Union's transport and industry sectors largely depends on upgraded power grids and storage capacity.
snippet: Electrifying the European Union's transport and industry sectors largely depends on upgraded power grids and storage capacity.

title: EU set to back return hubs in toughest migration crackdown yet
content: The EU is set to approve its controversial migration law, allowing return hubs outside the bloc, extending detention periods and introducing measures aimed at accelerating deportations – reflecting growing voter fears over migration.

title: Two former Syrian officials accused of torture during al-Assad era go on trial in Austria
content: Austrian law provides for the jurisdiction of local courts over certain offences committed abroad and the Vienna court has jurisdiction in this case because the defendants reside there.
snippet: Austrian law provides for the jurisdiction of local courts over certain offences committed abroad and the Vienna court has jurisdiction in this case because the defendants reside there.

title: At the weather's mercy: How many people in the EU can't afford air conditioning?

title: Hungarian government to amend constitution to allow removal of president

title: ‘Much better defence’ required to avert Russian drones, former Romanian NATO official tells Euronews
content: Exclusive: Former NATO Deputy Secretary-General Mircea Geoană has warned that  the military alliance needed stronger low-altitude military capabilities to shoot down drones, as seen with the incursion late last week in Romania that has left the country in "shock".

title: 'The United States scares me,' former Commission deputy boss Vera Jourová tells Euronews
content: Exclusive: Former European Commission Vice-President Vera Jourová said the current US administration "scares" her more than the challenges posed by Russia or China.
snippet: Exclusive: Former European Commission Vice-President Vera Jourová said the current US administration "scares" her more than the challenges posed by Russia or China.

title: Catching the unknown: The drone designed to hunt other drones

title: Should EU manufacturers use less Chinese parts? Take our poll
content: Brussels is considering limiting manufacturing supply chains. Instead of buying cheap, 100% Chinese components, European companies would be forced to source them from at least 3 different countries.
snippet: Brussels is considering limiting manufacturing supply chains. Instead of buying cheap, 100% Chinese components, European companies would be forced to source them from at least 3 different countries.

title: France and allies intercept uncompliant Russian shadow fleet tanker, Kremlin calls it 'piracy'
content: Russia has reportedly built up a flotilla of old oil tankers of opaque ownership to get around sanctions imposed by the European Union over Moscow's 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
snippet: Russia has reportedly built up a flotilla of old oil tankers of opaque ownership to get around sanctions imposed by the European Union over Moscow's 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

title: Europe Today: PSG's Champions League triumph marred by violence across France
content: Paris Saint-Germain's historic Champions League victory was marred by violence this weekend. As celebrations spread across France, so did clashes between football fans and police leading to nearly 800 arrests, more than 200 injured and one dead. Euronews' Jerry Fisayo Bami reports on this story.

title: Economy: why Poland is booming while eastern Germany falls behind

title: Accounts linked to EU-sanctioned figures displayed monetisation tools after sanctions, report finds

title: Who is behind the suspected sabotage attempts targeting the German navy?
content: Ten suspected sabotage incidents have reportedly impacted the German navy over the last year.

title: Flights temporarily suspended at Munich Airport after reported drone sighting
content: Munich Airport closed twice within 24 hours in October following suspected drone sightings.

title: EU expands sanctions on Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad to include Hamas Politburo members

title: Zelenskyy warns Russia may be preparing 'massive' new attack
content: It comes after Russia deployed its nuclear-capable Oreshnik missile in a large attack on the Kyiv region last weekend.
